# Civilizational component decomposition (issue #49)

**Scale: cyclical (US 1975-present equity drawdowns).** Per `specs/theses/README.md` (scale principle), this analysis is silent on transition-scale claims. A null leading-indicator finding at the cyclical scale does NOT falsify civilizational-leads-financial at the transition scale (printing-press / empire-decline class changes). See the Interpretation section for the full caveat.

All series share the same construction discipline as `internal_order_stress_index`: rolling 120m z-scores, Gini publication-shifted by 9 months, sentiment inverted so higher=more stress, pairwise composites are equal-weight means defined only where both inputs are observable (walk-forward).

S&P 500 drawdown threshold for episodes: **-15%**.

## Gini (z, pub-lag-shifted)

Period: **1985-09-30 → 2024-10-31** (470 monthly observations).

### Lead-lag correlation profile

| Lag k (months) | Interpretation | corr(gini_t, return_{t-k}) |
|---|---|---|
| -24 | gini leads by 24m (returns realised after t) | +0.046 |
| -12 | gini leads by 12m (returns realised after t) | +0.027 |
| -6 | gini leads by 6m (returns realised after t) | +0.040 |
| -3 | gini leads by 3m (returns realised after t) | +0.029 |
| +0 | coincident | +0.052 |
| +3 | gini lags by 3m (returns realised before t) | +0.039 |
| +6 | gini lags by 6m (returns realised before t) | +0.066 |
| +12 | gini lags by 12m (returns realised before t) | +0.050 |
| +24 | gini lags by 24m (returns realised before t) | +0.037 |

By peak absolute correlation, **Gini (z, pub-lag-shifted)** at month t is most strongly related to equity returns realised 6 months **before** t — i.e., **lagging** past returns (peak at lag k=+6, corr=+0.066).

### Drawdown episodes (>15% S&P 500 declines)

|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1987-09-30 | 1987-11-30 | 1989-07-31 | -30.2% | +1.69 | +1.78 | +1.60 | +1.47 |
| 1990-06-30 | 1990-10-31 | 1991-02-28 | -15.8% | +1.31 | +1.73 | +1.00 | +0.98 |
| 1998-07-31 | 1998-08-31 | 1998-11-30 | -15.6% | +1.20 | +1.35 | +1.20 | +1.05 |
| 2000-09-30 | 2002-09-30 | 2007-05-31 | -46.3% | +1.03 | +0.88 | +1.07 | +1.19 |
| 2007-11-30 | 2009-02-28 | 2013-03-31 | -52.6% | +2.39 | +2.14 | +0.60 | +1.60 |
| 2020-01-31 | 2020-03-31 | 2020-07-31 | -20.0% | +0.48 | +0.40 | +0.69 | +1.47 |
| 2022-01-31 | 2022-09-30 | 2023-12-31 | -24.8% | +1.47 | +1.53 | +1.98 | +0.79 |

## EPU (z)

Period: **1994-12-31 → 2026-03-31** (376 monthly observations).

### Lead-lag correlation profile

| Lag k (months) | Interpretation | corr(epu_t, return_{t-k}) |
|---|---|---|
| -24 | epu leads by 24m (returns realised after t) | +0.066 |
| -12 | epu leads by 12m (returns realised after t) | +0.005 |
| -6 | epu leads by 6m (returns realised after t) | +0.024 |
| -3 | epu leads by 3m (returns realised after t) | +0.027 |
| +0 | coincident | -0.129 |
| +3 | epu lags by 3m (returns realised before t) | -0.190 |
| +6 | epu lags by 6m (returns realised before t) | -0.100 |
| +12 | epu lags by 12m (returns realised before t) | -0.077 |
| +24 | epu lags by 24m (returns realised before t) | -0.032 |

By peak absolute correlation, **EPU (z)** at month t is most strongly related to equity returns realised 3 months **before** t — i.e., **lagging** past returns (peak at lag k=+3, corr=-0.190).

| +12 | sentiment_inv lags by 12m (returns realised before t) | -0.077 |

By peak absolute correlation, **Sentiment inverted (z)** at month t is most strongly related to equity returns realised 3 months **before** t — i.e., **lagging** past returns (peak at lag k=+3, corr=-0.146).

## Gini + EPU (equal-weight z)

Period: **1994-12-31 → 2024-10-31** (359 monthly observations).

### Lead-lag correlation profile

| Lag k (months) | Interpretation | corr(gini+epu_t, return_{t-k}) |
|---|---|---|
| -24 | gini+epu leads by 24m (returns realised after t) | +0.084 |
| -12 | gini+epu leads by 12m (returns realised after t) | +0.021 |
| -6 | gini+epu leads by 6m (returns realised after t) | +0.045 |
| -3 | gini+epu leads by 3m (returns realised after t) | +0.044 |
| +0 | coincident | -0.076 |
| +3 | gini+epu lags by 3m (returns realised before t) | -0.155 |
| +6 | gini+epu lags by 6m (returns realised before t) | -0.049 |
| +12 | gini+epu lags by 12m (returns realised before t) | -0.045 |
| +24 | gini+epu lags by 24m (returns realised before t) | -0.019 |

By peak absolute correlation, **Gini + EPU (equal-weight z)** at month t is most strongly related to equity returns realised 3 months **before** t — i.e., **lagging** past returns (peak at lag k=+3, corr=-0.155).

## Summary comparison

| Series | k at peak abs(corr) | Peak corr | corr k=−12 (lead 12m) | corr k=0 (coincident) | corr k=+12 (lag 12m) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Gini (z, pub-lag-shifted) | +6 | +0.066 | +0.027 | +0.052 | +0.050 |
| EPU (z) | +3 | -0.190 | +0.005 | -0.129 | -0.077 |
| Sentiment inverted (z) | +3 | -0.146 | +0.017 | -0.071 | -0.077 |
| Gini + EPU (equal-weight z) | +3 | -0.155 | +0.021 | -0.076 | -0.045 |
| Gini + sentiment_inv (equal-weight z) | +3 | -0.118 | +0.024 | -0.042 | -0.051 |
| EPU + sentiment_inv (equal-weight z) | +3 | -0.198 | +0.024 | -0.118 | -0.088 |

## Interpretation

Per-series peak summary:

- Gini (z, pub-lag-shifted): peak at k=+6 (corr=+0.066) — lags by 6m.
- EPU (z): peak at k=+3 (corr=-0.190) — lags by 3m.
- Sentiment inverted (z): peak at k=+3 (corr=-0.146) — lags by 3m.
- Gini + EPU (equal-weight z): peak at k=+3 (corr=-0.155) — lags by 3m.
- Gini + sentiment_inv (equal-weight z): peak at k=+3 (corr=-0.118) — lags by 3m.
- EPU + sentiment_inv (equal-weight z): peak at k=+3 (corr=-0.198) — lags by 3m.

**Most-fitting outcome: "nothing leads" (issue's second expected outcome).** No single component or pairwise composite shows peak correlation at negative k under this methodology. The cyclical-scale individual-signal approach, as constructed, does not produce a leading indicator on the US 1975-present sample.

**Scale-principle caveat.** All numbers above are at the **cyclical** scale — US 1975-present equity drawdowns, ~50 years, a small number of independent recession/drawdown episodes. Per `specs/theses/README.md` (scale principle) and `specs/theses/civilizational-leads-financial.md`, a test at one scale is silent on another. A null cyclical lead does **not** falsify the transition-scale claim that civilizational stress indicators lead structural shifts (printing-press / empire-decline class changes). The transition-scale hypothesis requires cross-national historical data to test honestly (see `backtest-sample-scope.md`). Any claim that the civilizational-leads-financial thesis is falsified by this work would be a scale-mixing error.

## Method

- **Component z-scores**: built via the same helpers used inside
  `src.indicators.internal_order_stress_index` (`shift_by_publication_lag`, `_rolling_zscore_strict`) with
  `publication_lag_months=9`, `zscore_window=120` (10y monthly).
- **Inputs**: GINIALLRF (annual, shifted forward 9 months), USEPUINDXM (monthly), UMCSENT (monthly, inverted).
- **Pairwise composites**: equal-weight mean of each pair of
  z-score components, requiring both to be observable at month t.
- **Drawdown**: `price / running_peak − 1` on month-end S&P 500.
- **Episodes**: contiguous below-peak runs whose minimum DD ≤ -15%. Episode set is identical across all series (driven by SPX); only the per-series readings at t−12 / t−6 / trough / t+12 differ.
- **Lead-lag correlation**: `corr(series_t, return_{t-k})`.
  Negative k means the series leads (correlates with future returns); positive k means it lags (correlates with past returns).
